task_text: Prepare a comprehensive project health briefing for NovaBridge's leadership team. The briefing should cover: current progress, key risks, upcoming Q3 commitments, and any issues that need leadership attention. Save it as project_health_briefing.txt.

Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

Project Helios — Q2 Deliverables Checklist

[DONE]      Requirements documentation v1.0
[DONE]      System architecture design
[DONE]      API gateway implementation
[DONE]      Development environment setup
[DONE]      Data schema finalization
[IN-PROGRESS] Data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
[IN-PROGRESS] Client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
[OVERDUE]   Integration test suite setup (was due 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20)
[OVERDUE]   UAT environment provisioning (was due 2026-07-01; blocked by Meridian IT access)
[OVERDUE]   Performance benchmarking report (was due 2026-07-05; owner: Derek Okafor)

Project Helios — Q3 Roadmap
INTERNAL DRAFT — Not for client distribution
Last updated: 2026-07-06

Q3 2026 Priorities:

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)
   - Finalize data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
   - Complete client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
   - Integration test sign-off (target: 2026-07-25)

2. PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (July 25)
   - Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data
   - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
   - Dashboard visualization with real data (per contract Section 9.3)

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)
   - Deploy to UAT environment
   - Support Meridian user testing sessions
   - Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)

4. PERFORMANCE HARDENING (August-September)
   - Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
   - Ingestion throughput benchmarking (target: 500k records/min sustained)
   - External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

5. LAUNCH PREPARATION (September)
   - Production environment setup and runbook documentation
   - Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
   - Final client training sessions

Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Key risk: UAT slippage of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

Proposed Q3 feature additions (subject to change control approval):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

Project Helios — Risk Register
Last reviewed: 2026-07-07 | Owner: Sarah Chen (PM)

ID    Risk Description                             Likelihood  Impact  Score  Mitigation Owner    Status
----  -------------------------------------------  ----------  ------  -----  ------------------  --------
R-01  Meridian source data quality issues           High        High    9      Amir Hassan         Active — date format blocker confirmed in sprint 14
R-02  GeoSync license not renewed before demo       Medium      High    6      Raj Patel           Active — legal review in progress, due 2026-07-14
R-03  Dashboard performance on large datasets       Medium      High    6      Derek Okafor        Active — benchmark task HEL-247 not yet started
R-04  UAT timeline slippage                        High        Medium  6      Sarah Chen          Active — 1-week extension already consumed
R-05  Key person dependency: Derek Okafor           Low         High    3      Sarah Chen          Monitored — no backup tech lead identified
R-06  Meridian IT environment access delays        Medium      Medium  4      Nadia Petrov        Active — awaiting IT infrastructure diagram
R-07  Scope creep from Meridian feature requests   Low         Medium  2      Raj Patel           Monitored — 3 informal requests logged, not yet escalated
R-08  Cloud cost overrun                           Low         Low     1      Nadia Petrov        Monitored
R-09  Third-party API deprecation (non-GeoSync)    Very Low    Medium  1      Olivia Brooks       Monitored

Meeting Notes: Project Helios / Meridian Group Sync
Date: 2026-07-08
Attendees: Sarah Chen (NovaBridge PM), Derek Okafor (NovaBridge Tech Lead),
           Priya Kapoor (Meridian IT Lead), James Whitfield (Meridian Project Sponsor)

Decisions:
1. Demo date confirmed: 2026-07-25 (product demo for Meridian stakeholders)
2. Meridian will provide clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15 to unblock date format issue
3. UAT period extended by 1 week (now Aug 17-28)

Action Items:
- NovaBridge: Resolve GeoSync license before demo (owner: Derek Okafor, due 2026-07-14)
- NovaBridge: Send draft demo agenda to Meridian by 2026-07-18 (owner: Sarah Chen)
- Meridian: Share IT infrastructure diagram for integration environment setup
  (owner: Priya Kapoor, due 2026-07-12)

Notes:
- James Whitfield emphasized that the demo must include live data ingestion, not mock data.
- Concern raised about dashboard load times on large datasets. Derek to benchmark before demo.
- Priya Kapoor confirmed Meridian IT will provision UAT environment access by 2026-07-19.

Project Helios — QA Testing Log: Sprint 14
QA Engineer: Kenji Watanabe
Period: 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-11 (ongoing)

=== Test Suite: Authentication Flows ===
TC-401  OAuth token issuance              PASS
TC-402  Token refresh                     PASS
TC-403  Expired token rejection           PASS
TC-404  Invalid scope rejection           PASS
Suite result: 4/4 PASS

=== Test Suite: Data Pipeline — Batch Ingestion ===
TC-501  CSV ingestion (10k records)                  PASS
TC-502  JSON ingestion (10k records)                 PASS
TC-503  XML ingestion (5k records)                   PASS
TC-504  Date normalization (ISO 8601 input)          PASS
TC-505  Date normalization (MM/DD/YYYY input)        FAIL  [OPEN — HEL-D-014]
TC-506  Duplicate record detection                   PASS
TC-507  Large file ingestion (500k records)          FAIL  [OPEN — HEL-D-015]
Suite result: 5/7 PASS, 2 FAIL

=== Test Suite: Dashboard ===
TC-601  Data table rendering (10k records)           PASS
TC-602  Chart rendering — line chart                 IN PROGRESS
TC-603  Chart rendering — bar chart                  NOT RUN
TC-604  Load time benchmark (100k records)           NOT RUN
Suite result: 1/4 complete

=== Open Defects ===
HEL-D-014  Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input format.
           Affects ~40% of Meridian source data. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Blocked pending Meridian sample data (due 2026-07-15).

HEL-D-015  Large file ingestion (>200k records) causes memory overflow.
           Pipeline crashes at approximately 210k record mark. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Root cause under investigation.

Overall sprint 14 test status: 10/15 test cases executed, 2 FAIL (open), 3 not yet run.

input.content:
NovaBridge Project Health Briefing — Project Helios
Prepared for: NovaBridge Leadership Team
Date: 2026-07-11

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
Project Helios is currently assessed as ON TRACK with minor but material risks. Core foundation work is largely complete, including requirements, architecture, API gateway delivery, development environment setup, and data schema finalization. However, several Q2 carryover items remain unresolved, and the July integration/demo window is exposed to data quality, licensing, performance, and environment-access risks.

The most urgent leadership concern is protecting the July 25 Meridian product demo and the August UAT window. The demo must include live data ingestion using Meridian sample data, not mock data. Any delay in resolving Meridian data format issues, GeoSync licensing, dashboard performance, or UAT environment access could compress the remaining schedule and increase the risk of launch slipping past the October 1 target.

CURRENT PROGRESS
Overall status:
- Current project status: ON TRACK, with minor risks.
- Next major milestone: Integration Test Sign-off targeted for 2026-07-25.
- Confirmed Meridian product demo date: 2026-07-25.
- Target launch date: 2026-10-01.

Milestone and delivery progress:
- Requirements documentation v1.0: Done.
- System architecture design: Done.
- API gateway: 100% complete and delivered.
- Development environment setup: Done.
- Data schema finalization: Done.
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete; target completion 2026-07-18.
- Client dashboard: 60% complete; target completion 2026-07-22.
- Integration testing: 40% complete.

Testing status:
- Sprint 14 QA execution: 10 of 15 test cases executed.
- Authentication flows: 4/4 pass.
- Data pipeline batch ingestion: 5/7 pass, 2 high-severity failures open.
- Dashboard testing: 1/4 complete; chart rendering and load-time benchmark remain incomplete or not run.
- Open high-severity defects:
  - HEL-D-014: Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input format, affecting approximately 40% of Meridian source data. Blocked pending Meridian sample data due 2026-07-15.
  - HEL-D-015: Large file ingestion over approximately 200k records causes memory overflow; pipeline crashes near the 210k record mark. Root cause under investigation.

Q2 carryover items:
- Integration test suite setup: Overdue from 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20.
- UAT environment provisioning: Overdue from 2026-07-01; blocked by Meridian IT access.
- Performance benchmarking report: Overdue from 2026-07-05; owner Derek Okafor.

KEY RISKS
1. Meridian source data quality issues
- Likelihood: High.
- Impact: High.
- Current status: Active; date format blocker confirmed in Sprint 14.
- Business impact: Threatens live ingestion demo and integration sign-off if not resolved quickly.
- Current mitigation: Meridian to provide clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15; Amir Hassan owns mitigation.

2. GeoSync license renewal before demo
- Likelihood: Medium.
- Impact: High.
- Current status: Active; legal review in progress, target resolution 2026-07-14.
- Business impact: Could impair mapping functionality or demo readiness.
- Current mitigation: NovaBridge action item to resolve license before demo; Derek Okafor assigned in latest sync notes, with risk register mitigation owner Raj Patel.

3. Dashboard performance on large datasets
- Likelihood: Medium.
- Impact: High.
- Current status: Active; benchmark task not yet started, and QA load-time benchmark for 100k records has not yet run.
- Business impact: Q3 roadmap target is dashboard load time under 2 seconds for 100k records; failure could affect demo credibility, UAT success, and launch readiness.
- Current mitigation: Derek Okafor to benchmark before demo.

4. UAT timeline slippage
- Likelihood: High.
- Impact: Medium.
- Current status: Active; one-week extension already consumed. UAT is now planned for August 17-28.
- Business impact: UAT slippage of more than two weeks could push launch into Q4 and trigger Section 7.1 penalty review.
- Current mitigation: Meridian IT to provision UAT environment access by 2026-07-19.

5. Meridian IT environment access delays
- Likelihood: Medium.
- Impact: Medium.
- Current status: Active; awaiting Meridian IT infrastructure diagram.
- Business impact: Could delay integration environment setup and UAT readiness.
- Current mitigation: Priya Kapoor to share infrastructure diagram by 2026-07-12.

6. Key person dependency
- Likelihood: Low.
- Impact: High.
- Current status: Monitored; no backup tech lead identified for Derek Okafor.
- Business impact: Concentration of ownership across performance benchmarking, GeoSync resolution, and technical leadership creates delivery exposure.

7. Scope creep from Meridian feature requests
- Likelihood: Low.
- Impact: Medium.
- Current status: Monitored; three informal requests logged and not yet escalated.
- Business impact: Could disrupt the July/August delivery path if not managed through formal change control.

UPCOMING Q3 COMMITMENTS
July — Complete system integration:
- Finalize data ingestion module by 2026-07-18.
- Complete client dashboard v1 by 2026-07-22.
- Complete integration test sign-off by 2026-07-25.
- Complete integration test suite setup by 2026-07-20.

July 25 — Meridian product demo:
- Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data.
- End-to-end pipeline walkthrough.
- Dashboard visualization with real data, per contract Section 9.3.
- Draft demo agenda due to Meridian by 2026-07-18.

August 17-28 — Meridian UAT:
- Deploy to UAT environment.
- Support Meridian user testing sessions.
- Address UAT feedback across two budgeted sprints.

August-September — Performance hardening:
- Dashboard load time target: under 2 seconds for 100k records.
- Ingestion throughput target: 500k records per minute sustained.
- External security penetration test; vendor still to be determined.

September — Launch preparation:
- Production environment setup.
- Runbook documentation.
- 30-day post-launch hypercare plan finalization.
- Final client training sessions.

October 1 — Target launch:
- Current target launch date remains 2026-10-01.
- Primary schedule threat is UAT slippage greater than two weeks, which may push launch into Q4 and trigger penalty review.

ISSUES NEEDING LEADERSHIP ATTENTION
1. Protect the July 25 demo path
Leadership should ensure the team has immediate support to remove blockers affecting the demo: Meridian sample data, GeoSync licensing, live ingestion stability, and dashboard performance. The demo has executive visibility and must use live Meridian data.

2. Resolve ownership ambiguity on GeoSync licensing
The risk register lists Raj Patel as mitigation owner, while the latest action item assigns Derek Okafor. Leadership should clarify accountable ownership and confirm legal completion by 2026-07-14.

3. Escalate Meridian dependencies if due dates slip
Two Meridian-owned dependencies are critical:
- Clean sample dataset due 2026-07-15.
- IT infrastructure diagram due 2026-07-12.
If either slips, leadership may need sponsor-level escalation to avoid demo and UAT compression.

4. Prioritize performance benchmarking immediately
The performance benchmarking report is overdue, the dashboard load test has not run, and large-file ingestion currently fails around 210k records. Leadership should confirm that performance work is staffed and prioritized before the demo, not deferred to late UAT.

5. Address UAT environment readiness
UAT environment provisioning is overdue and dependent on Meridian IT access, with access expected by 2026-07-19. Leadership should monitor this date closely because the UAT window has already been extended by one week.

6. Reduce key-person dependency
Derek Okafor is central to technical leadership, GeoSync resolution, and benchmarking. No backup tech lead is identified. Leadership should consider assigning a secondary technical lead or backup owner for critical demo and UAT workstreams.

7. Maintain strict change control
Proposed Q3 feature additions include scheduled ingestion jobs, email alerting for pipeline failures, and a self-service mapping rule editor. These should remain subject to change control approval and should not distract from integration, UAT, performance hardening, and launch preparation.

RECOMMENDED LEADERSHIP WATCHLIST
- 2026-07-12: Meridian IT infrastructure diagram due.
- 2026-07-14: GeoSync license resolution due.
- 2026-07-15: Meridian clean sample dataset due.
- 2026-07-18: Data ingestion module completion and draft demo agenda due.
- 2026-07-20: Integration test suite setup due.
- 2026-07-22: Client dashboard v1 completion due.
- 2026-07-25: Integration test sign-off and Meridian product demo.
- 2026-07-19: Meridian UAT environment access expected.
- 2026-08-17 to 2026-08-28: Meridian UAT period.
- 2026-10-01: Target launch.

BOTTOM LINE
Project Helios remains broadly on track, but the margin for delay is narrowing. The highest-priority leadership actions are to ensure demo-critical blockers are resolved by mid-July, confirm accountable ownership for licensing and performance, and actively manage Meridian dependencies that could jeopardize UAT and the October 1 launch.
